# Muffin Framework — Full Documentation

Muffin is a client-side Web Components framework built by Footloose Labs. It has two packages:
- `@muffin/element` — core: DOMComponent, PostOffice, createStore, Router
- `@muffin/atom-websdk` — network layer: WebRequestSdk, Service, DOM extensions

Both ship as a single CDN bundle. After loading, everything is available under `window.Muffin`.

Current stable version: atom-websdk 3.1.7 / element 0.9.3
CDN: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/FootLooseLabs/atom-websdk@3.1.7/dist/sdk.min.js

---

# Getting Started

Muffin is a client-side platform built on Web Components. It has two layers:

| Package | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `@muffin/element` | Core — DOMComponent, PostOffice, createStore, Router, Lexeme |
| `@muffin/atom-websdk` | Network layer — WebRequestSdk, Service, DOM extensions |

Both ship as a single CDN bundle (`sdk.min.js`) or as separate ES modules.

## CDN

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/FootLooseLabs/atom-websdk@3.1.6/dist/sdk.min.js"></script>
```

After loading, `window.Muffin` is available with everything:

```js
const { DOMComponent, PostOffice, createStore, Router, WebRequestSdk } = window.Muffin
```

## Vite project (ESM)

```bash
pnpm add @muffin/element @muffin/atom-websdk
```

## Defining a component

```js
class MyCard extends Muffin.DOMComponent {
    static domElName = 'my-card'

    static markupFunc(_data, uid, uiVars) {
        return `
            <div class="card">
                <h2>${uiVars.title}</h2>
                <p>Count: ${uiVars.count}</p>
                <button on-click="increment">+</button>
            </div>
        `
    }

    constructor() {
        super()
        this.uiVars.title = 'Hello'
        this.uiVars.count = 0
    }

    increment(srcEl, ev) {
        this.uiVars.count++   // triggers re-render automatically
    }
}

MyCard.compose()
```

```html
<my-card></my-card>
```

---

# Components

Every muffin component extends `Muffin.DOMComponent` (which extends `HTMLElement`). The component IS the DOM element — no wrapper, no virtual node.

## Defining a component

`markupFunc` and `stateSpace` are **static**. Instance state lives in `constructor()` via `this.uiVars`.

## Registration

`.compose()` registers the Web Component and wires up the Muffin internals.

Batch composition — a parent composes its children:

```js
AppUI.compose = () => {
    SideNav.compose()
    MainContent.compose()
    AppUI.prototype.constructor._composeSelf()
}
AppUI.compose()
```

## Lifecycle

| Method | When |
|---|---|
| `constructor()` | Set `uiVars`, `stores`, `transitionSpace` here |
| `onConnect()` | After first render. Good for subscriptions and async data loading. |
| `disconnectedCallback()` | When removed from DOM. Always call `super.disconnectedCallback()`. |

## Passing data via attributes

```html
<user-card data='{"id": 1, "name": "Ankur"}'></user-card>
```

Available as `_data` in `markupFunc`. Default shape declared via `static schema`.

## Event binding

Attach handlers via `on-*` attributes. Handlers receive `(srcEl, ev)`.

Supported: `on-click`, `on-change`, `on-input`, `on-keydown`, `on-keyup`, `on-focus`, `on-blur`, `on-scroll`, `on-dblclick`, `on-load`, `on-contextmenu`.
`on-contextmenu` automatically calls `ev.preventDefault()`.

## Conditional rendering

```js
return `
    <p render-if="uiVars.loading">Loading…</p>
    <p render-if="!uiVars.loading">${uiVars.message}</p>
`
```

False = `display:none`, element stays in DOM.

## XSS protection

`esc()` is available in every `markupFunc`:

```js
return `<p>${this.esc(uiVars.userInput)}</p>`
```

## Derived (computed) state

```js
static derived = {
    isEmpty: (uiVars, data) => uiVars.items.length === 0,
    visibleCount: (uiVars, data) => uiVars.items.filter(i => i.visible).length
}
```

Evaluated fresh on every render, merged into `uiVars`. Read-only — never set derived keys directly.

## Unique instance ID

Each instance gets a `uid`. Use it to scope IDs:

```js
return `
    <label for="input-${uid}">Name</label>
    <input id="input-${uid}" type="text" />
`
```

## styleMarkup

Scoped styles per component. `rootEl` is a CSS selector scoped to this instance:

```js
static styleMarkup(rootEl, currentState) {
    return `<style>
        ${rootEl} { position: relative; }
        ${rootEl} .spinner { display: ${currentState === 'loading' ? 'block' : 'none'}; }
    </style>`
}
```

---

# Reactive State (uiVars)

`uiVars` is a Proxy-wrapped object. Setting any property automatically schedules a batched re-render.

## Auto-render on mutation

```js
this.uiVars.count++   // schedules render automatically
```

Multiple mutations in the same call produce one render (batched via `queueMicrotask`).

## Forcing an immediate render

```js
this.uiVars.loading = true
this.render()   // flush now, before awaiting async work
```

## Shallow proxy

The Proxy is **shallow** — mutating a nested property won't trigger a render. Reassign the top-level key:

```js
// Does NOT trigger render
this.uiVars.user.name = 'Ankur'

// Correct
this.uiVars.user = { ...this.uiVars.user, name: 'Ankur' }
```

## uiVars vs stores

| | `uiVars` | `createStore()` |
|---|---|---|
| Scope | Local to one component instance | Shared across any number of components |
| Persistence | No | Optional (IndexedDB) |
| Use for | UI state, loading flags, form input | Session, domain data, anything shared |

---

# Stores

`createStore()` creates a named reactive store. Any component that declares a store re-renders automatically when it changes.

## Creating a store

```js
const sessionStore = Muffin.createStore('session',
    { user: null, token: null },
    {
        persist: true,            // save to IndexedDB, rehydrate on load
        socket: 'WebInterface',   // PostOffice interface to sync with
        socketLabel: 'session-update'
    }
)
```

## Using in a component

```js
constructor() {
    super()
    this.stores = { session: sessionStore }
}

static markupFunc(_data, uid, uiVars, routeVars, _constructor, stores) {
    if (!stores.session.user) return `<span>Not logged in</span>`
    return `<span>${stores.session.user.name}</span>`
}
```

## Store API

```js
store.get()                      // → current state snapshot
store.set({ user: userData })    // partial merge, notifies all subscribers
store.reset()                    // resets to initial state
const unsub = store.subscribe(state => { ... })
```

---

# State Machine

Every component has a built-in state machine via `static stateSpace` and instance `transitionSpace`.

## Defining states

```js
static stateSpace = {
    idle:       { apriori: ['loading', 'error'] },
    loading:    { apriori: ['idle'] },
    error:      { apriori: ['loading'] },
}
```

`apriori` — states allowed to transition INTO this state.

## Transition hooks

```js
constructor() {
    super()
    this.transitionSpace = {
        'idle <to> loading': () => this.loadData(),
        'loading <to> error': () => { this.uiVars.errorMsg = 'Failed' }
    }
}
```

## Switching state

```js
this.switchState('loading')
this.switchToIdleState()   // alias for switchState('idle')
```

## composedScope after switchState

`switchState` schedules a render via `queueMicrotask` — the new markup is not in the DOM synchronously. Child elements in the new state's markup are not yet mounted in `composedScope` immediately after the call.

Use `awaitChildLoad` — it resolves deterministically when the child's `connectedCallback` fires:

```js
// WRONG — composedScope.inputBar not mounted yet
this.switchState('loaded')
this.composedScope.inputBar.setData(data)

// CORRECT
this.switchState('loaded')
await this.awaitChildLoad('inputBar')
this.composedScope.inputBar.setData(data)
```

---

# PostOffice

Cross-component messaging layer. Handles local pub/sub and wraps WebSocket connections into named interfaces.

## Interfaces

```js
// Publish
Muffin.PostOffice.sockets.global.dispatchMessage('user-selected', { id: 42 })

// Subscribe (inside a component)
this.interface.on('user-selected', (msg) => {
    this.uiVars.selectedId = msg.id
})
```

## Advertising an interface

```js
class NotificationDialog extends Muffin.DOMComponent {
    static domElName = 'notification-dialog'
    static advertiseAs = 'NotificationManager'

    onConnect() {
        this.advertisedInterface = Muffin.PostOffice.getOrCreateInterface('NotificationManager')
        this.advertisedInterface.on('notify-foreground', (msg) => {
            this.uiVars.message = msg.msgTxt
        })
    }
}
```

## PostOffice vs stores

| | PostOffice | createStore() |
|---|---|---|
| Nature | Events — transient, fire and forget | State — persistent, readable at any time |
| Use for | Notifications, actions, socket messages | Session data, domain lists, shared UI state |

---

# WebSocket SDK

`atom-websdk` extends element with `WebRequestSdk` — a structured layer over WebSocket.

## Interface address syntax

| Separator | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `host:::Name` | Receptive | Request → Response |
| `host\|\|\|Name` | Expressive | Subscription / push |

## Making requests

```js
const res = await Muffin.WebInterface.request(
    '@host/app:::AgentService',
    { subject: 'getDetails', params: { id: 'abc123' } },
    { MAX_RESPONSE_TIME: 10000 }
)
```

## Subscriptions

```js
await sdk.websubscribe('@host/app|||broadcast-event', 'global', 'broadcast-label')
Muffin.PostOffice.sockets.global.on('broadcast-label', (msg) => { ... })
```

## Service pattern

```js
class AgentService extends Muffin.Service {
    static name = 'AgentService'
    static Interfaces = { Main: '@vritti/ideator:::AgentDevelopmentService' }

    static async getDetails(id) {
        const res = await Muffin.WebInterface.request(
            this.Interfaces.Main,
            { subject: 'getDetails', params: { slug: id } },
            { MAX_RESPONSE_TIME: 10000 }
        )
        return res.result
    }
}
```

`Service` base provides TTL caching, interface locking (no duplicate in-flight requests), and subscription locking (no duplicate listeners).

**Important:** Services are static-only. `this.interface` does not exist in static methods. To subscribe and forward to a consumer component, pass the consumer's interface name as a string:

```js
static subscribeToUpdates(consumerInterfaceName) {
    Muffin.WebInterface.subscribe(this.Interfaces.Main, consumerInterfaceName, { subject: 'update' })
}

// in component onConnect():
MyService.subscribeToUpdates(this.interface.name)
this.interface.on('update', (msg) => { this.uiVars.data = msg })
```

---

# API Reference — DOMComponent

Base class for all muffin components. Extends `HTMLElement`.

## Static properties

| Property | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `static domElName` | `string` | Custom element tag name. Required. |
| `static markupFunc` | `function` | Returns HTML string. Called on every render. |
| `static stateSpace` | `object` | State machine definition. |
| `static derived` | `object` | Computed state. Functions of `(uiVars, data)`. |
| `static schema` | `object` | Default shape for `_data`. |
| `static styleMarkup` | `function` | Returns CSS string. |
| `static advertiseAs` | `string` | PostOffice interface registration name. |

## Instance properties (set in constructor)

| Property | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `this.uiVars.*` | `any` | Reactive local state. Any assignment triggers batched render. |
| `this.stores` | `object` | Named stores. `{ key: storeInstance }`. Changes trigger re-render. |
| `this.transitionSpace` | `object` | State transition hooks. `{ 'from <to> to': fn }`. |

## markupFunc signature

```js
static markupFunc(_data, uid, uiVars, routeVars, _constructor, stores)
```

## Dynamic attributes on child components

When a parent re-renders and changes an attribute on a child Muffin custom element, the child does **not** automatically re-render. There is no `observedAttributes` equivalent.

**Pattern: inline rendering**

```js
// Instead of passing a changing attribute to a child (child won't update):
`<lucide-icon icon="${uiVars.isPlaying ? 'pause' : 'play'}"></lucide-icon>`

// Render inline — parent reconciler replaces output wholesale:
`${LucideIcon.renderIcon(uiVars.isPlaying ? 'pause' : 'play', 20)}`
```

## composedScope

`composedScope` is a named registry — nothing more. Children register themselves by key when they connect to the DOM. The parent accesses them by key to call methods.

```js
// child registers itself (automatic — via childscope attribute)
// parent accesses:
this.composedScope.sidebar.loadData()
```

Parent rendering does NOT affect child rendering. Each component has its own independent render cycle.

---

# API Reference — DOM Extensions

Applied to every component automatically by `applyAtomWebSDK()`.

## Element queries

### `this.getElement(selector)`
First matching element within the component's rendered DOM. Scoped — does not search the full document.

```js
postRender() {
    const input = this.getElement('input[name="title"]')
    input.focus()
}
```

### `this.getElements(selector)`
All matching elements as an array.

**Prefer these over raw `querySelector`.** `_getDomNode()` is an internal method and not part of the public API.

## UI helpers

### `this.toggleBtnBusyState(btnEl, state?)`
Add/remove `busy` class and `disabled` attribute on a button.

### `this.toggleRootAttr(name, value?)`
Toggle an attribute on the component root element. Passing `false` or a falsy value **removes** the attribute entirely.

### `this.toggleSurface(surfaceName, state?, toggleClass?)`
Show/hide a named `surface=""` element.

### `this.awaitChildLoad(childscope, timeout?)`
Returns a Promise that resolves when the named child component has rendered. Default timeout: 5000ms.

```js
async onConnect() {
    await this.awaitChildLoad('detailPanel')
    this.composedScope.detailPanel.loadData()
}
```

### `this.initSubscriptions(subscriptions)`
Batch-wire WebSocket subscriptions. Cleans up automatically on disconnect.

### `this.callParent(srcEl, ev)`
Invoke a method on the nearest ancestor component. Method name from `srcEl`'s `pass-method` attribute.

---

# API Reference — createStore()

```js
const store = Muffin.createStore(name, initialState, options?)
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | `string` | Unique store name |
| `initialState` | `object` | Initial state values |
| `options.persist` | `boolean` | Persist to IndexedDB. Rehydrates on load. |
| `options.socket` | `string` | PostOffice interface for socket sync |
| `options.socketLabel` | `string` | Message label on that socket |

Return value: `{ get(), set(partial), reset(), subscribe(fn) }`

---

# API Reference — Service

Base class for SDK service layers. Always static — no instantiation.

```js
class MyService extends Muffin.Service {
    static name = 'MyService'
    static Interfaces = { Main: '@host/app:::ServiceInterface' }

    static async getData(id) {
        const res = await Muffin.WebInterface.request(
            this.Interfaces.Main,
            { subject: 'getData', params: { id } },
            { MAX_RESPONSE_TIME: 10000 }
        )
        return res.result
    }
}
```

## Built-in behaviour

```js
static getCached(key)               // → value or null
static setCached(key, value, ttl?)  // default ttl = 60s
static clearCache(key)

static async lockInterface(name, throttle?)     // prevent duplicate in-flight requests
static unlockInterface(name)

static async lockSubscription(name, throttle?)  // prevent duplicate listeners
static unlockSubscription(name)
```

---

# API Reference — PostOffice

```js
PostOffice.getOrCreateInterface(name, specs?)
PostOffice.publishToInterface(name, message)
PostOffice.addGlobalListener(name, handler)
PostOffice.sockets.global.dispatchMessage(label, payload)
```

## PostOffice.Socket

```js
const socket = new Muffin.PostOffice.Socket({ url: 'wss://...', interfaceName: 'main' })
socket.connect()
```

Auto-reconnects, keep-alive ping every 59s.

---

# API Reference — WebRequestSdk

```js
const sdk = new Muffin.WebRequestSdk(config)
await sdk.connect()
```

| Method | Description |
|---|---|
| `connect()` | Open WebSocket. Auto-reconnects. |
| `request(interface, message, opts?)` | Request → Response |
| `websubscribe(interface, socket, label, opts?)` | Persistent subscription |
| `communicate(message)` | Fire-and-forget |

`Muffin.WebInterface` is the shared singleton initialized at app boot.

---

# Patterns — Muffin + Tailwind + Vite

Standard project structure:

```
src/
  components/
    app-ui/app-ui.js
    sidebar/sidebar.js
    index.js          ← all .compose() calls
  scripts/
    1_touchpoint.js   ← CDN script load
    2_utilities.js
    3_app.js          ← Router, WebInterface, root component
    4_polyfills.js    ← DOMComponent.prototype extensions
  styles/main.css
  main.js
```

`3_app.js` initializes Router, WebInterface, and the root component. `components/index.js` calls `.compose()` on every component. See full pattern in docs.

---

# Patterns — React + Muffin

Muffin as WebSocket/service transport layer only, React owns the UI.

```
React component → hook → Service class (extends Muffin.Service) → Muffin.WebInterface → WebSocket
```

Load Muffin before React mounts, initialize `Muffin.WebInterface`, then use service classes from React hooks exactly as in muffin-only projects. No `DOMComponent` subclasses needed in the React layer.

Services extend `ElementWebService` (the TypeScript-compatible base class from `atom-websdk`) rather than `Muffin.Service`, and use a `WebInterfaceProvider` singleton to access the authenticated `WebInterface` instance.

```ts
import { ElementWebService } from '../muffin-sdk'
import { WebInterfaceProvider } from './WebInterfaceProvider'

export default class AccountManagementService extends ElementWebService {
    static name = 'account-management-service'

    static Interfaces = {
        SessionServiceInterface: '@flpl.atom/authentication-handler:::SessionService',
    }

    static async getSessionProps(): Promise<unknown> {
        const interfaceName = 'SessionServiceInterface'
        await this.lockInterface(interfaceName)
        try {
            const response = await WebInterfaceProvider.request(
                this.Interfaces[interfaceName],
                { subject: 'getSessionProps', object: {} },
                { MAX_RESPONSE_TIME: 5000 }
            )
            return (response as { result: unknown }).result
        } finally {
            this.unlockInterface(interfaceName)
        }
    }
}
```

---

# Patterns — Managing Services Across Projects

As a muffin-based org grows across multiple projects, the same services tend to get copy-pasted and drift out of sync. The recommended pattern is a **private services monorepo** for the org.

## Structure

```
your-org-services/   (private repo)
  packages/
    services-ts/       ← services for React + Muffin projects (TypeScript)
    services-vanilla/  ← services for vanilla Muffin projects (plain JS)
    sdk/               ← WebInterfaceProvider and other infra (not services)
```

Each package is a plain directory of service files with a `registry.json` manifest — no build step required.

## registry.json

Each package needs a `registry.json` for `muf services` discovery:

```json
{
  "services": {
    "AccountManagementService": {
      "description": "Account info, session props, wallet, subscriptions",
      "stack": "ts",
      "tags": ["account", "wallet", "subscriptions"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Consuming via muf

Configure `.mufrc.json` in each project root:

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "services": [
      {
        "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/your-org/your-services/main/packages/services-ts/registry.json",
        "stack": "ts",
        "alias": "@org-services",
        "path": "../../your-services/packages/services-ts"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Then:

```sh
muf services list                        # list all available services
muf services add AccountManagementService  # checks/prints vite alias + import line
```

## Manual vite alias (no CLI)

```js
// vite.config.js
resolve: {
    alias: {
        '@org-services': path.resolve(__dirname, '../../your-services/packages/services-ts')
    }
}
```

```json
// tsconfig.json
{ "compilerOptions": { "paths": { "@org-services/*": ["../../your-services/packages/services-ts/*"] } } }
```

```ts
import AccountManagementService from '@org-services/AccountManagementService'
```

## What belongs in the monorepo

Move a service in when it appears in more than one project with minimal divergence. Services tied to a single project's domain stay local under `src/muffin-services/` or `src/web-services/`. The monorepo supplements — it does not replace — project-local services.

---

# CLI — .mufrc.json (Private Registries)

`muf` supports private org registries for components, templates, and services alongside the public FootLooseLabs registries. Place `.mufrc.json` in your project root (or any ancestor — `muf` walks up from cwd).

## Full config shape

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "components": [
      "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/your-org/your-components/main/registry.json"
    ],
    "templates": [
      "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/your-org/your-templates/main/registry.json"
    ],
    "services": [
      {
        "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/your-org/your-services/main/packages/services-ts/registry.json",
        "stack": "ts",
        "alias": "@org-services",
        "path": "../../your-services/packages/services-ts"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Private entries are merged with the public registry — private wins on name collision. For private GitHub repos, set `GITHUB_TOKEN` in the environment — picked up automatically for all fetches.

## Services config fields

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `url` | yes | URL to the `registry.json` in your services repo |
| `stack` | no | `"ts"` or `"vanilla"` — shown in list output |
| `alias` | no | Import alias (default: `@hais-services`) |
| `path` | no | Relative path used in vite alias snippet |
| `token` | no | Per-registry token — prefer `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var |

## Per-command behaviour

| Command | Public registry | Private registries |
|---------|----------------|-------------------|
| `muf list` | FootLooseLabs/muffin-components | merged in |
| `muf list --templates` | FootLooseLabs/muffin-templates | merged in |
| `muf add <name>` | fetches from public repo | fetches from private repo |
| `muf services list` | — | reads configured services registries |
| `muf services add <name>` | — | checks vite alias, prints import line |

---

# Migration — Upgrading to v3.0.0

Key breaking changes from v2:

1. **`Muffin.Service` is now built-in** — remove local `ElementWebService` polyfill
2. **`uiVars` is now a reactive Proxy** — `render()` after assignments is optional but safe
3. **DOM helper methods are built-in** — remove all `DOMComponent.prototype.*` assignments from polyfill files
4. **Global utility extensions are built-in** — remove `Array.prototype.splitIntoMultipleArrays` and `String.prototype.ellipsify`
5. **`window.Muffin` is the single namespace** — bare globals (`window.DOMComponent` etc.) removed

New in v3: `createStore()`, `static derived`, `esc()` in markupFunc.

---

# Migration — Component Migration v2 → v3

Key patterns:

1. **Reactive uiVars** — assignment auto-schedules render; no need to call `render()` manually
2. **Nested mutations** — always replace the top-level key, never mutate nested properties in-place
3. **`renderSelectively()`** — no-op without `data-uivar` nodes; remove dead calls
4. **Non-template state** — move DOM refs and intermediate values to plain instance properties
5. **`childscope`** — required for stateful children to survive parent re-renders
6. **`getParent()` timing** — only valid in `onConnect()` or `postRender()`, not constructor
7. **No Shadow DOM** — do not call `attachShadow()`

---

# Known Issues

Real-world issues from projects using Muffin. Includes resolved items for reference.

| # | Area | Issue | Workaround | Status |
|---|------|--------|------------|--------|
| 1 | Event binding | `on-keydown`, `on-focus`, `on-blur`, `on-dblclick` were missing from supported `on-*` attribute list. | Bind imperatively via `addEventListener`. | Resolved in atom-websdk 3.1.5 |
| 2 | Service static `this.interface` | `this.interface` is `undefined` in static service methods. | Pass consumer interface name as string to `Muffin.WebInterface.subscribe()`. See Service docs. | Not a bug — expected JS behaviour. Documented. |
| 3 | Child component attribute reactivity | Changing an attribute on a child Muffin component from the parent does not trigger child re-render. No `observedAttributes` equivalent. | Render dynamic output inline in the parent template. See DOMComponent docs. | Architectural limitation — documented. |
| 4 | Store subscriptions not firing | `this.stores` declared in constructor did not register reactive subscriptions. `_subscribeToStores()` ran before subclass constructor assigned `this.stores`. | `store.subscribe(() => this.render())` in `onConnect()`. | Resolved in element 0.9.3 / atom-websdk 3.1.6 |
| 5 | `composedScope` after `switchState` | Children in new state markup not yet in `composedScope` synchronously after `switchState`. | Use `await this.awaitChildLoad('key')` — deterministic, event-driven. | Architectural behaviour — documented. |
| 6 | Boolean attribute removal on re-render | Boolean attributes (`disabled`, `readonly`) set via template expression not removed when condition flips. DOM patcher only calls `setAttribute`, never `removeAttribute`. | Manage toggling boolean attributes imperatively in `postRender`. | Confirmed bug — fix pending. |
| 7 | `on-load` for cached resources | Handler attached too late for cached images — `load` fires synchronously during DOM insertion before event binder runs. | `addEventListener('load', handler, { once: true })` imperatively; check `el.complete` first. | Confirmed timing bug — fix pending. |
| 8 | `getElement` / `getElements` undiscoverable | Methods existed but were not in public API docs. Developers fell back to `_getDomNode().querySelector()`. | Use `this.getElement(selector)` and `this.getElements(selector)`. `_getDomNode()` is internal. | Docs gap — resolved. |
